news.title: Banking Bonanza Tournament
news.body:
Register Here------> https://forms.gle/1yv8KvpRgg4MzQ4a7 

Rules:

  • Players will lag to see who breaks.
  • If you pocket a ball on the break you spot it and keep shooting.
  • You cannot combo or kick at balls in the pocket.
  • Shots must be made clean, if not it will go on the spot.
  • If you scratch when making a ball, you lose the ball you made and it gets put on the spot.
  • If you scratch the cue ball goes behind the headstring.
  • If you do not hit a rail or pocket a ball AFTER contact with the object ball it is a foul.
  • Use your highest SKL in 8 or 9 with the MAX being a 7.

news.body:
Right now we have 1 division in harlingen and 1 in mcallen. IF we have 10 teams In each division, they will each send a team to Vegas! The regional event will be held in the city you compete in. You must start as a SKL 10 together. YOu play one INDIVIDUAL match and then a doubles match. This is truly your best shot at vegas COMPETING against the least amount of teams!

EDITH- Edinburg- Tuesday

EDDIE- Mcallen- Tuesday

news.title: Team Captain Tournament Rules and Registration
news.body:
Register Here--------> https://forms.gle/Ta4UZNhoXvvRt4NH9 

1. A maximum of 5 players allowed on roster. 2. A player’s highest skill level (SL) in this tournament will be the highest of either the player’s skill level in 8-Ball or 9-Ball, unless the player is a SL8 or a SL9 in either format in which case their skill level in this tournament will be a SL7. 3. Teams choose 3 of the 5 team members to participate in each match. The total of the skill levels of the 3 players fielded cannot exceed 15. Only one player of skill level 6 or higher, sometimes referred to as a Senior Skill Level Player, can be fielded. 4. It is permissible for a team to count the skill level of an eligible player who is on the roster but not present at the match site towards the Team Skill Level Limit. 5. An ineligible player’s skill level may not be used towards the Team Skill Level Limit. 6. Teams that cannot comply with the Team Skill Level Limit of 15, will play two players whose combined skill levels do not exceed 11, and forfeit the vacant match. 7. Team Captains flip a coin to decide who must declare their three players for the match first. Winner of the flip has the choice of declaring first or having the opposing Team Captain choose first. 8. The players are listed in ascending order (lowest to highest). That list determines the player rotation, and is locked for the match. Once both teams in a match have declared their players, the players cannot be changed unless the combined skill levels of the three players declared by a team exceed the maximum permitted combined skill level in which case the team will be allowed to declare three different players prior to first rack being struck. 9. Players of same skill level may be placed in order of preference. 10. If a team only has two eligible players for a match, the player with the highest skill level must be in Player Spot 3. The remaining player must be in Spot 2. 11. Teams with only two eligible players will play a race to 5. 12. Teams with only one eligible player will forfeit the team match. 13. Players lag to see who wins the first break. 14. Alternate between 8-Ball & 9-Ball racks, starting with 8-Ball. 15. Each individual rack is worth one team point. 16. Once the first two players have completed their rack of 8-Ball, the second two players in the rotation will play a rack of 9-Ball. Upon that rack’s completion, the next two players in the rotation will play a rack of 8-Ball. Once set, the player rotation continues until a team reaches the required points necessary to win the match. 17. If a player is not present for any game in the rotation, they forfeit all subsequent games in their rotation spot. 18. For a 9-Ball game, the individual rack is over when the 9-ball is legally pocketed. 19. The winning team breaks with the next player in the rotation after each rack. 20. Each team is limited to one coaching time-out per game. 21. It is a loss of game to shoot out of rotation. Although it is sportsmanlike for the non-offending team to remind the other team that they are about to play a player out of rotation, they are not required to do so. However, before any infraction has occurred, the team that has posted a player out of rotation may avoid penalty by asking the non-offending team which player’s turn it is in the rotation. In that circumstance, the team must tell them the truth, and the correct player in the rotation will assume their rightful spot in the game. Once the offending team has taken a shot out of rotation, the infraction has occurred, the game is over, and the sitting team will receive one point for the rack. Both teams will then move to the next game in the match, with players in the correct rotation. However, if the game is completed without the non-offending team calling the infraction, and the next rack is broken, the results of previous rack will stand, and the team that shot out of rotation will assume the new rotation for the remainder of the match.

news.title: Merry Christmas and Happy Holidays!!
news.body: Wishing you a very special holiday with you and your family! 😁

news.title: Reminder: Christmas Party!!!
news.body:
I hope to see all your faces here for this event! It is such a fun time in the year! I love the way we all come together and celebrate friends we have made and the joy pool brings to us!

BUT we all must remember a potluck isnt a potluck if you dont brings some of the pots! LOLOL., I am bringing:

-3 brisket

-pasta salad

-jalepeno corn salad

BUT we still need more. If your team can pitch in together and bring something it will be appreciated. <3 

Captains: I will be contacting you to let you know if you player recieved an end of year award!

Players: There is a toys for tots bin if you feel like donating an unwrapped toy of $5 value or more. AND there is a white elephant if you want to participate in the timeless classic of getting stuff you dont want and stealing from others! LOL 

Raffle tickets will be given out per person at the event! This year as a special bonus.... we are giving a ticket out per team you are on. If you are on 5 teams you get 5! So please come and get your tickets the moment you arrive. 

So much stuff and so much fun! Can't WAIT for this weekend!

news.title: 9 Ball Doubles Winners!
news.body:
OOOOOOOOOOH This Sunday was so much fun! With our biggest field yet (in 9 Ball) we had 23 awesome teams! I'm super happy to announce the winners! Congratulations to The Alliance!! I would also like to add that since they dressed up they won BEST DUO! Which took a $100 Prize! So they participated for free and WON! I would also like to congratulate Los Compas for a great showing as well. The first place total was $2300!!!!

8 Ball Doubles is next weekend! Get your partner and I'll see you there!

news.title: Banking Bonanza Tournament
news.body:
register here------> https://forms.gle/PPfDNAhSfj8KVLL5A 

Rules: 

-Players will lag to see who breaks.

-If you pocket a ball on the break you spot it and keep shooting. 

-You cannot combo or kick at balls in the pocket. 

-Shots must be made clean, if not it will go on the spot.  

-If you scratch you lose a ball and the ball you made and the ball you lost get put on the spot. 

-If you scratch the cue ball goes behind the headstring. 

-If you do not hit a rail or pocket a ball AFTER contact with the object ball it is a foul and you lose a ball. 

-Use your highest SKL in 8 or 9 with the MAX being a 7.

news.title: Schedules are UP!
news.body:
The schedules are ready BUT the rosters are still being adujsted. Please contact the captain of the team if you wish to schedule your matches for a different time. 

Harlingen and Brownsville: Don't forget your start time is at 7:00pm until the curfew changes. Captains- its YOUR responsibility to let the opposing team know if you are wanting to reschedule any of your 5 matches. PLEASE WORK TOGETHER TEAMS. All of us are having to deal with the limitations of COVID. Let's not be hard on each other.

As always if you have any questions please feel free to call me anytime. 956-821-6925

news.title: Team TShirt Contest
news.body:
1. All artwork MUST BE orignal. DO NOT use any trademarked images

2. This contest is open to ACTIVE APA of the RGV teams ONLY.

3. All entries must be submitted electronically to erin_cano@yahoo.com or to 956-821-6925 (DO NOT POST THEM TO FACEBOOK)

4. One or more submissions per team is acceptable.

Determination of the Winning Logo:

All submitted logos will be voted on through a google forms on Monday March 30 through Tuesday March 31 til 7:00 pm. The logo with the most votes will WIN!

Be Creative and Have Fun!

For more info call Erin Lacy @

956-821-6925

news.title: VOTE HERE!!
news.body:
Everyone place your votes here. Remember each winning picture will get a $50 gift card to the bar of their choice!! Good Luck to everyone!

https://forms.gle/a2Cr43sytrqD2WZL7

news.title: Having Fun At APA League Night
news.body:
This is a small contest we are having to help lighten and brighten the mood. Join in the fun! Good luck to all who enter! 

https://www.facebook.com/APARGV/

news.title: Important announcement about league play
news.body:
APA of the RGV- Important Announcement about League Play

Attention players:

As of this moment, due to an abundance of caution for your health and well-being, we will be postponing League and tournament play for the next 2 weeks.

Over these next 2 weeks we will assess the situation and proceed forward in a way that protects the players and their families. I will be posting more information throughout these next 2 weeks. So please keep a look out for additional information. We are a family and we will remain so.

Thank you for your understanding and patience through this time.

- Erin Lacy League Operator APA of the RGV

news.title: Thanksgiving Break!
news.body:
Happy Thanksgiving!! We are off this week. There are NO make up matches that need to be played. Enjoy this time with your friends and family.

Best Wishes!

Erin Lacy

news.body: 9 ball Doubles tournament for vegas will start at 10 am @Eddy's Tavern in McAllen. Please notifiy your teammates!!

news.title: Billiard Golf and Free Cash Cup Registration Forms
news.body:
Ok! Its that time! Register HERE for the  

Free Cash Cup Tournament and the Billiard Golf Tournament

Here are the rules for the tournaments:

- 3 person roster 

- 3 players must stay under 15

- ALL PLAYERS MUST HAVE 10 LIFETIME 

 MATCHES

- Single Modified Bracket (Cash Cup)

- first 32 teams that register (Cash Cup)

- first 36 teams that register (Billiard Golf)

- I will close the registration 3 days after I 

 open it whether it's filled out or not.

- This event uses your highest skill level. 

 with a 7 max.

- Only one skill level 6 or higher on each 

 team

-Players MUST be active on an 8 ball or 9 

 ball team

Billiard Golf Tournament

https://forms.gle/vYDTYCGTvcQ2UejeA 

Free $2000 Cash Cup Tournament

https://forms.gle/DC975DqtN6hF7i1g9

news.title: Mid-South Invitational Qualifier
news.body:
Please fill out the form below to register for Mid-South Invitational Qualifier. The teams that win this tournament will compete in the Mid-South invitational that will happen in November. The prize pool for that event is $12,000!!! First will take $7,000! WOW! That event will also be held at EDDY's Tavern in Mcallen.

There is a $50 charge per team which will go to the winning team. It is a Modified-Single Elimination 16 team bracket. If we get 24 teams that register I will have (2) 12 team brackets. I will be sending 2 spots this year and this event is first come first serve. This is for summer session teams ONLY. 

Here is the link:

https://forms.gle/UTgmqUzzhQQJMyq3A

news.title: SINGLE BOARD CHANGE
news.body: I know we had a ton of players request their own weekend tournaments. BUT we didn't have enough turn-out these last boards. So we will be going back to the once a month single boards. We are offering incentives to players who play on more than one board and we will try to make sure you are as close to your skill levels as possible. So for any inconvenience this may cause. If you have any questions you may call me anytime. Erin Lacy 956-821-6925

news.title: Fourth of July!
news.body:
Hey ALL!

I have released the ppwk for the 4th of July. Please contact the teams that you are playing to make up the match if you are going to be doing something that night.

news.title: Cash Cup Teams!!!!
news.body:
HEY GUYS!! WE HAVE OUR 32 TEAMS!!!! EVERYONE SIGNED UP IN LESS THAN 24 HOURS!!!

Due to high demand for this event:

We are going to be having a FREE $2000 EVENT in the FALL and SPRING SESSION TOO! So don't worry if you didn't make into this event this time. Every year we will guarantee the SUMMER SESSION FREE $2000 EVENT. This year we are able to do more! PLUS, all the the WPCQ teams can play in those events! Now, the rules will stay the same. Teams need to add up to 15 with 3 players and need to have at least 10 lifetime matches. 

For this event, I will be allowing players to change only 2 members from this list. Updated rosters will be posted on facebook and the app.

So far APA of the RGV has given out $30,000 in cold hard cash to VALLEY PLAYERS!!! AND we still have more events to come for the year!! We are also sending 8 different teams to Vegas this year not just 1! Sometimes when things sound too good to be true they are but not in this case. There is a reason we are the BIGGEST and BEST in the Valley. Good luck to everyone in the event and keep your EYES OPEN for the live bracket drawing!

Skrilla

Just the balls

Shoot to chill

Anything is possible

news.title: Single Boards
news.body: Ok Everyone!! Single board dates are posted!! If you want to compete please look at the dates and locations. Each weekend we are offering single boards for specific skill levels. Good luck to everyone participating. As always, if you have any questions about these events please feel free to contact me anytime. 956-821-6925

news.title: WORLD QUALIFIER MEETING
news.body:
The World Qualifier meeting will happen on May 11, 2019 @ 10 am at Eddy's Tavern. I will be holding the best of the rest that day. Any captain playing in the best of the rest will also have to attend this meeting. We will be discussing the following:

  • Team Certification Form
  • Tournament Rules
  • Start time & End times for the event
  • Prize Money

As a captain you do not NEED to be present but you do need to send a member from your team. IT IS MANDATORY. This is where you will get your form that needs to be turned in to be BEFORE the event. I would also advise you to make sure your whole team is present the following week for league. EVERY ONE of your members HAS to sign this form. If you have an questions or concerns please feel free to contact me anytime.

news.title: MASTERS & LADIES TIME CHANGE
news.body:
Attention Teams!

The Masters and Ladies tournament will be starting at a different time. Registration will start at 11:00 and play will start at 12:00. Your team NEEDS to be present no later than 11:30. So please have your memebers there on time. If you have a few players there that is fine. BUT you will need to be able to pay your entry into the event in FULL. If you have any questions for concerns please feel free to contact me anytime. 956-821-6925. I look forward to seeing y'all this weeking!

news.title: Player Meeting
news.body: Attention Team Captains!!! We will be having a meeting for Cameron County on April 6th @ Creasy's at 12:00 PM and for Hidalgo County on April 7th @ Eddy's Tavern at 12:00 PM. Each team NEEDS a representative present. Captains can go to either meeting. Both will be having single boards afterwards. If you are interested in making a team you should attend as well. We will be going over all of the information for the new year.

news.title: PLAYER APPRECIATION SIGN IN FORM
news.body:
Sign in form here

https://goo.gl/forms/fYUCl9g3OlcNmICf2

news.title: New 1MIN Timers!
news.body: Hey Guys!! We now have 1 min timers to help keep track during time outs. Please wait till the coach has gotten to the table BEFORE you start the time. You can also use them in between matches to put ppl up. But REMEMBER that it is 2 minutes to put up each player. GOOD LUCK and HAVE FUN!

news.title: Mid-Valley Here We Come!
news.body:
We are expanding to the mid valley!! If any of yall know of some people or bars that might be interested please have them call me as soon as possible. There are a few bars looking for some extra ppl to get their teams started. Here are some of the locations:

Ice House Sports Bar and Grill

Mustang Lounge

Jungle Inn

Brews and Cues

Golden Cue

Los Gallos

So like I said before, if you know anyone looking for a good time PLAYING POOL 😀 send them my way! 😉

news.title: FREE RAFFLE TICKETS
news.body: HEY GUYS!! If you are looking for some extra tickets for the raffle if you register you will get one. Please text me as soon as you do and I will send one your way! We are giving away a trip to VEGAS for the World Poolplayer championship. The more tickets you have to better your chances. Along with other door prizes of course. 😁 But this is the main one. Good Luck!

news.title: DOUBLES FOR VEGAS ANYONE?
news.body:
IF you are interested in going to Vegas here is another chance! There are 3 ways you can get there with a partner. Below you will see the links to sign up for the event. If you look for the event in the calendar you will see the rules and requirements to enter this event. Each event usually gets about 14-19 teams. Thats $1400- $1900 prize pool!!! Just remember your team has to equal 10!

Good Luck!!

9 Ball Doubles

https://goo.gl/forms/G57BRgh0RIweLcNJ2

8 Ball Doubles

https://goo.gl/forms/Rsc7PiREsT3rF3uW2

Jack and Jill

https://goo.gl/forms/cUaTz3rZJHVJVtLu1

As always call me with any questions!

956-821-6925 Erin Lacy

news.title: $2000.00 CASH CUP
news.body: ATTENTION TEAMS!!! The cash cup is coming up next month. You want to make sure your captain is following APA of the RGV on FACEBOOK. This tournemant is first come first serve. Each team entry fee is $100 and is for the first 24 teams that reigster online. I will be sending out an alert on Facebook and that's when you need to register. Payouts for this event are : 1st place $3000.00 and 2nd plce $1400.00. You do have to be an active summer 8 ball team and it NEEDS to be your captain that registers the team. Please contact me if your captain doesn't have Facebook. The tournament is a single modified elimination tournament and will run for 3 days. The last 2 teams will not be allowed to split. There will be matches that are live steamed as well. So prepare your team. GOOD LUCK to all participants!!!
